Full job description
Regional Director of Healthcare Operations – Sue Ryder North East and Yorkshire
Based at both Manorlands Hospice in Keighley and Wheatfields Hospice in Leeds
Salary: Circa £95k + Benefits
Contract Type: Permanent

Lead, Inspire, and Transform Care

In person interviews to take place early/mid May

Are you an exceptional leader with a passion for driving change and delivering high-quality healthcare services? Sue Ryder is seeking a dynamic and visionary Regional Director of Healthcare Operations, to oversee our healthcare service in the North East and Yorkshire and lead our strategy for growth across the region.

About Us

Sue Ryder is a national charity providing expert care for people with life-limiting conditions. We operate across seven regions, working closely with healthcare professionals, local authorities, and voluntary organisations to ensure individuals receive compassionate and person-centred support.

Sue Ryder is here to make sure everyone approaching the end of their life or living with grief can access the support they need. There is no one size fits all when it comes to how we cope and the help we need, but with our support, no one has to face dying or grief alone.

We are there when it matters

The Role

As the Regional Director of Healthcare Operations, you will be at the forefront of our mission, playing a pivotal role in shaping and delivering innovative models of care. You will be responsible for the overall performance, operational delivery, and financial management of your healthcare region, ensuring that services are safe, effective, and continuously improving.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading and developing a high-performing, inclusive, and compassionate team.
Driving the implementation of our new PEoLC strategy to create a sustainable and impactful care ecosystem.
Establishing and maintaining strong relationships with NHS partners, commissioners, and the voluntary sector to enhance service delivery.
Ensuring financial and operational excellence, meeting regulatory and governance requirements.
Acting as a visible and inspiring leader, championing our values and mission at all levels.

About You

We are looking for a strategic, results-driven leader with:

A postgraduate-level qualification or equivalent experience in healthcare or the third sector.
A strong background in senior healthcare leadership, operational management, and service transformation.
Exceptional relationship-building and negotiation skills, with a proven ability to influence key stakeholders.
Financial and strategic planning expertise, ensuring sustainable and high-quality care delivery.
A commitment to inclusive leadership, fostering a culture of collaboration, learning, and continuous improvement.
A kind, patient-centred attitude and approach with a strong focus on addressing inequalities, diversity, and inclusion.

Why Join Us?

At Sue Ryder, we offer more than just a job – we provide an opportunity to make a lasting impact. In return for your leadership, passion, and expertise, we offer:

Company pension scheme
Continuation of NHS pension (T&Cs apply)
- 27 days holiday - rising to 33 with length of service plus bank holidays
NHS annual leave honoured (T&Cs apply)
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay
Enhanced sick pay
- Employee Networks - LGBTQ+, Ethnic Diversity and Equality, People with Disabilities, and Women and Non Binary Individuals
Staff discount of 10% on new goods online at shop.sueryder.org
Structured induction programme and learning and development opportunities.

For more of our employee benefits please visit our website.

https://www.sueryder.org/jobs/why-work-for-sue-ryder

Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion

At Sue Ryder, we recognise that a diverse workforce allows us to provide the best care and support. We are committed to encouraging equity, diversity and inclusion among our workforce, and eliminating unlawful discrimination.

We welcome applications from people from Black, Asian and Minority Ethnic communities, LGBTQ+ individuals, those from lower socio-economic backgrounds, and those living with disabilities.

As a Disability Confident Committed employer, we’re proud to support the ‘Offer an Interview’ scheme. This means we will offer an interview to all disabled applicants who best meet the minimum essential criteria for the role.

We use inclusive recruitment practices including sharing interview themes or questions in advance, offering remote interviews where needed, and other accessibility support.

If you require support to compete an application or participate fully in the interview process, please email recruitment@sueryder.org.

Once in post you’ll have access to staff and volunteer networks for LGBTQ+ colleagues, Global Cultures, people with disabilities, and women and non-binary individuals, plus an Inclusion Passport to record and carry your workplace adjustments.

For more information on our Equity, Diversity and Inclusion work, please visit: www.sueryder.org/jobs/equity-diversity-inclusion

Join us in creating a culture where everyone feels respected, valued, and able to thrive.
location: United Kingdom; based at Manorlands Hospice, Keighley and Wheatfields Hospice, Leeds
shortCompanyReason: National charity providing care for people with life-limiting conditions and grief support.
shortRoleReason: Senior regional leadership role overseeing healthcare operations, strategy, performance, and finance.
companySummary: Sue Ryder is a national charity delivering expert care for people with life-limiting conditions and support for those facing grief.
roleSummary: The role leads healthcare operations across the North East and Yorkshire, with responsibility for performance, delivery, financial management, service transformation, and stakeholder relationships.
headlineAttraction: Lead regional healthcare operations for a national charity with a strong mission and EDI commitment.
rolePurpose: To shape and deliver innovative, safe, effective, and sustainable healthcare services across the region.
advertSummary: Sue Ryder is hiring a Regional Director of Healthcare Operations to lead and develop healthcare services across the North East and Yorkshire. The post focuses on operational excellence, financial management, strategy implementation, partnership working, and inclusive leadership.
personalisedIntro: I’ve led operational change in healthcare and other regulated settings, with a focus on service quality, performance control and practical improvement. This role fits the kind of work I do best: taking responsibility for delivery, tightening the numbers, and helping teams perform well in a live service environment.
whyThisRole: This role needs someone who can hold the operational line while also improving how services work over time. I’d bring experience of service turnaround, reporting, financial control and frontline leadership, along with the ability to work with partners and keep delivery focused on patient need.
companyPridePoints: National charity with expert care focus
coreResponsibilities: Lead and develop a high-performing team
Manage regional performance and operational delivery
Build relationships with NHS partners, commissioners, and the voluntary sector
Champion Sue Ryder values and mission
essentialRequirements: Focus on inequalities, diversity, and inclusion
impliedStrategicGoals: Create a sustainable and impactful care ecosystem
possibleHeadlineFacts: Based at Manorlands Hospice, Keighley and Wheatfields Hospice, Leeds
Company pension and NHS pension continuation mentioned

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Senior operations leader who brings structure, financial grip and calm delivery to complex services.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’ve led operational change in healthcare and other regulated settings, with a focus on service quality, performance control and practical improvement. This role fits the kind of work I do best: taking responsibility for delivery, tightening the numbers, and helping teams perform well in a live service environment.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: I’m drawn to Sue Ryder because the mission is clear and the work is grounded in real care for people facing life-limiting conditions and grief. The fact that Sue Ryder operates across seven regions, with a visible commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion, tells me this is an organisation that expects leaders to balance performance with compassion and consistency.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This role needs someone who can hold the operational line while also improving how services work over time. I’d bring experience of service turnaround, reporting, financial control and frontline leadership, along with the ability to work with partners and keep delivery focused on patient need.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: patient-centred service improvement and operational performance
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I turned around patient experience in a live NHS primary care setting, which is directly relevant to improving service quality in healthcare operations.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest healthcare proof point near the top of the page.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I improved my NHS primary care Google score from 2.09 to 4.51 and lifted five-star reviews from 19.8% to 81.3%.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: operational management, resilience and performance discipline
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations through a scale-up phase, which shows I can keep delivery steady while the service model is changing.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting evidence for operational leadership and control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I helped strengthen margin, orders won and operational resilience while the business scaled.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: team leadership, workload control and stakeholder coordination
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve led a team through business-critical work in a regulated environment, which is useful where pace, standards and coordination all matter.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for leadership style and delivery discipline.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I led and supported a team of 12 while maintaining service standards in a high-security environment.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role needs someone who can combine regional oversight with hands-on operational judgement. It is not just about keeping services running; it is about improving performance, protecting quality, managing money carefully and building the relationships that make delivery sustainable.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’m currently leading operational performance and transformation in NHS primary care, with responsibility across clinical, administrative and patient-facing functions.
experienceMappings.relevance: This is the closest direct match to the healthcare leadership base the role asks for.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operational improvement in a specialist manufacturing scale-up, where process, capacity and resilience all needed tightening as the business grew.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can work through change without losing control of delivery.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation, released £33.3k of working capital and helped increase cash at bank by £172.6k year on year.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role needs someone who can keep services sustainable, not just effective.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I built senior reporting across workflow, room utilisation, payroll and contractual income, including reconciliation of 64 PCSE remittance files covering £2.83m.
experienceMappings.relevance: This is relevant to the governance and performance discipline expected in a regional leadership role.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led and supported a 12-person function through annual business-critical work, keeping service standards and training in view at the same time.
experienceMappings.relevance: The brief asks for a compassionate, inclusive leadership style, and I’m used to balancing delivery with people management.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked on operational improvement that required practical coordination, implementation thinking and buy-in around a new monitoring system.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role will depend on working with NHS partners, commissioners and voluntary sector stakeholders.

focusAreasToBring.title: Regional performance control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on getting a clear view of service performance, pressure points and delivery risks, then using that to keep decisions practical and timely.

focusAreasToBring.title: Sustainable service improvement
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would look for changes that improve quality and flow without adding unnecessary complexity, so the service is stronger over time.

focusAreasToBring.title: Partnership working
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would build relationships carefully and keep them useful, because this role depends on trust with NHS, commissioner and voluntary sector partners.

focusAreasToBring.title: Financial discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on cost, income and working capital so service quality is supported by a sustainable operating model.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background lines up with this role because I’ve already worked across healthcare, regulated environments and operational change, and I’m comfortable being accountable for both delivery and improvement. I bring a mix of hands-on control, reporting discipline and people leadership that suits a regional role with real performance responsibility.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend my first months getting close to the service, understanding the regional picture and making sure performance, finance and governance are all visible. From there, I’d focus on the practical changes that support safer delivery, stronger partnerships and a more sustainable operating model across the region.
companyHighlights: Sue Ryder is a national charity with care and grief support at its core.
The organisation operates across seven regions.
Sue Ryder has a clear commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.
The advert highlights structured induction and learning and development opportunities.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people expect straight answers, steady follow-through and a focus on improvement. The compassionate, collaborative and learning-led tone in the advert matches how I like to lead.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would spend time understanding the regional operating picture, key stakeholders, current performance, financial position and the main delivery risks.
I would look to sharpen reporting, clarify priorities with the team and identify where small operational changes could improve flow, quality or control.
I would focus on embedding the agreed priorities, strengthening partner relationships and making sure the region has a clear, workable plan for performance and sustainability.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring steady leadership, clear operational judgement and a practical focus on making services work better for the people who rely on them.
closingProofPoints: I improved NHS primary care Google reviews from 2.09 to 4.51.
I supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation and released £33.3k of working capital.
I led and supported a 12-person function while maintaining service standards in a regulated environment.
contentNotes: I have kept company-specific claims limited to what the advert states.
I have used three evidence examples because they give the strongest spread across healthcare, operational control and leadership.
I have not added unsupported detail about Sue Ryder’s internal structure or current regional priorities beyond the advert.
The evidence bank is stronger on operational and healthcare delivery than on direct hospice leadership, so I have framed fit through transferable healthcare and regulated-service leadership.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
